data.title: Pelican Acquisition announces pricing of $75M initial public offering
data.description:
• Pelican Acquisition (PELIU) on Friday said it has priced its initial public offering of 7.5M units at $10.00 per unit on May 22, 2025.
  • The units are listed on the Nasdaq Global Market and trade under the ticker symbol “PELIU” beginning on May 23, 2025.
  • Each unit consists of one ordinary share of the company and one right, with each right entitling the holder thereof to receive one-tenth (1/10) of one ordinary share upon the consummation of an initial business combination.
  • Once the securities comprising the units begin separate trading, the ordinary shares and rights are expected to be listed on Nasdaq under the symbols “PELI” and “PELIR”, respectively.
  • The offering is expected to close on May 27, 2025.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: 5E Advanced Metals files to sell 2.02M shares of common stock by selling shareholders
data.description:
• 5E Advanced Metals (NASDAQ:FEAM) filed a prospectus related to the offer and resale of up to about 2.02 million shares of common stocks.
  • This prospectus is not an offer to sell.
  • The company will not receive any proceeds from the sale of ordinary shares by the selling shareholder.
  • Filing

data.title: Purple files to sell 92.64M shares of Class A common stock for holders
data.description:
• Purple Innovation (NASDAQ:PRPL) filed to sell 92.64M shares of Class A common stock for holders.
  • This prospectus is not an offer to sell.
  • Filing

data.title: Lake Shore Bancorp announces stock offering
data.description:
• Lake Shore Bancorp (NASDAQ:LSBK) announced the launch of its common stock offering as part of the proposed conversion from a mutual to a stock holding company.
  • The Bank will convert its charter to a New York commercial bank and be renamed Lake Shore Bank.
  • Up to 5.75M shares are being offered at $10/share.
  • Shares will be offered first to eligible depositors and the employee stock ownership plan, then in a community offering and potentially to the general public.
  • At least 4.25M shares must be sold for the conversion to proceed.

data.title: Silvercrest Asset Management Group announces $25 million common stock repurchase program
data.description:
• Silvercrest Asset Management Group (NASDAQ:SAMG) announced on Friday that the company's board of directors authorized a common stock repurchase program.
  • Under the program, the company may purchase up to $25 million of its Class A common stock, as market conditions warrant.
  • The shares may be repurchased through open market purchases, privately-negotiated transactions, block purchases, one or more 10b5-1 share trading plans, or otherwise in accordance with all applicable federal and state securities laws and regulations, at prices that the company deems appropriate.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Trump halts plans to create soverign wealth fund to tackle debt, Bessent says - report
data.description:
The Trump administration has halted plans to establish a sovereign wealth fund, opting instead to concentrate on reducing the national debt, Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent reportedly said on Friday.

“I think the president has decided it’s on pause while we work on everything else that we’re doing now,” Bessent said in an interview with Bloomberg Television. Trump "said the other day that we’ll probably spend more time paying down debt. He is laser focused on paying down debt.”

On Feb. 3, Trump signed an executive order to create the fund, fulfilling a campaign promise aimed at leveraging returns to "promote fiscal sustainability, lessen the burden of taxes, and establish long-term economic security."

Now, however, the White House is exploring a leaner investment approach -- one that leverages existing federal agencies and sidesteps the need for congressional approval.

Bessent and Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick submitted their recommended plans earlier this month, yet internal dissatisfaction led White House officials to withhold a final plan from reaching Trump's desk, Bloomberg reported, citing people familiar with the matter.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: US Steel surges as Trump says company will remain in U.S. in 'partnership' with Nippon
data.description:
US Steel (NYSE:X) shares jumped late in Friday's trading, closing +21.2%, after President Trump said the company would remain in the U.S., headquartered in Pittsburgh, in a "planned partnership" with Japan's Nippon Steel (OTCPK:NPSCY) (OTCPK:NISTF).

"This will be a planned partnership between United States Steel and Nippon Steel, which will create at least 70,000 jobs, and add $14 billion dollars to the U.S. Economy," with most of the investment occurring in the next 14 months, Trump said in a post on Truth Social.

The announcement came shortly after the White House confirmed Trump received a recommendation from the Committee on Foreign Investment in the U.S. that was reviewing the national security implications of the proposed merger of the two companies.

data.title: Sunnova Energy International receives NYSE non-compliance notice
data.description:
• Sunnova Energy International (NYSE:NOVA) received a notice from the New York Stock Exchange that NOVA is not in compliance as a result of its failure to timely file its quarterly report for the quarter ended March 31, 2025.
  • NOVA has until November 19, 2025, to file the quarterly report and regain compliance.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: Movano Health receives notification of deficiency from Nasdaq
data.description:
• Movano Health (NASDAQ:MOVE) on Friday said it has received a notice from Nasdaq indicating that because the company had not yet filed its Form 10-Q for the quarterly period ended March 31, 2025, it was not in compliance with listing rules requiring Nasdaq-listed companies to timely file all periodic financial reports with the SEC.
  • The notice states that the company has 60 calendar days, or until July 21, 2025, to submit a plan to regain compliance with the listing rule.
  • MOVE +1.85% after hours to $0.5602.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Wall Street sheds nearly 3% for the week on fiscal concerns, Trump's tariff threats
data.description:
Wall Street on Friday closed out a topsy-turvy week with losses, as concerns over fiscal debt and U.S. President Donald Trump's latest salvo of tariff threats weighed on sentiment.

Trade war developments were in the spotlight since the morning, after Trump threatened to impose tariffs on tech giant Apple (AAPL) and the European Union (EU). Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent later calmed investors somewhat, saying he expects "several, large" trade deals to be announced soon. 

The benchmark S&P 500 index (SP500) fell -0.67% to close at 5,802.82 points. The blue-chip Dow (DJI) slipped -0.61% to end at 41,603.07 points, and the tech-heavy Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) slid -1.00% to settle at 18,737.21 points.

For the week, the S&P retreated -2.61%, while the Dow and the Nasdaq lost -2.47% each. 

“Equity markets this week took a breather following the vertiginous climb up and out of the post-Liberation Day lows," Alex King, investing group leader of Cestrian Capital Research, told Seeking Alpha.   

"Today the opening gap down was bought up all day long, across the S&P, the Nasdaq, Dow and the Philadelphia SOX (SOX). We believe this portends higher-for-longer prices for stocks – yet another hated rally contra the prevailing narrative of Doom," King added.   

Earlier, the impact of Trump's announcements on his Truth Social service in the morning was immediate, with stock index futures plunging. 

First, Trump took aim at Apple (AAPL). He pitched a tariff of at least 25% on the tech giant's products if it did not build its iPhones in the U.S. Apple (AAPL) shares ended -3%. 

Then, Trump targeted the EU. After pointing out several of the EU's "unfair" practices, he asserted that trade discussions with the union were "going nowhere" and that he was recommending a straight 50% tariff on EU goods starting June 1.   

The EU is one of the biggest trading partners of the United States. According to the U.S. Trade Representative, total goods trade with the European Union were an estimated $975.9B in 2024.     

Bessent later in a Bloomberg TV interview urged market participants to focus on the United States' 18 other "important trading partners."

"My sense is, over the next couple of weeks, we're going to have several, large deals announced...I expect that we will be negotiating in person with (China) again," Bessent added.   

Turning to the fixed-income markets, U.S. Treasury yields continued to stabilize following a bruising bond sell-off on Wednesday. The benchmark 10-year yield (US10Y) was last down 2 basis points at 4.52%, while the shorter-end more rate-sensitive 2-year yield (US2Y) was little changed at 4.00%.

For more, see how Treasury yields have done across the curve on the Seeking Alpha bond page.

Of note, the U.S. stock market is shut on Monday for Memorial Day.
